pk-red: The cartridge that started a lifelong hunt. Pokémon Red shipped in 1996 and rewired what a handheld could be — 151 creatures, a link cable, and the maddening knowledge that you could never catch them all alone.
This copy is complete in box: outer sleeve, manual, and the red cartridge with its label barely sun-faded. It anchors the Kanto shelf.
lotr-hobbit: A timeless adventure that started it all. Before the Ring, there was a hobbit, a map, and a door round and green like a porthole.
Acquired four years ago at an estate sale, this hardcover is a cornerstone of the Tolkien shelf — and the reason the rest of the collection exists.
dune-book: Frank Herbert's desert epic, in a clothbound hardcover edition. Spice, sandworms, and the slow machinery of empire.
Added this month. It completes the original six-book arc on the shelf — though the question of which translation of the Appendices counts remains gloriously unresolved.
zelda-la: Link washes ashore on Koholint Island in this 1993 Game Boy classic, later reborn in clay-bright HD. A dream you don't want to end.
The cartridge joined the collection in May, slotting neatly beside the rest of the portable Zelda line.
bladerunner: Denis Villeneuve's 2017 continuation, owned on 4K Blu-ray. Rain, neon, and the ache of synthetic memory.
Logged this month — and, per your own honesty, still unwatched. The disc waits.
morgan-1884: An 1884 Morgan Dollar struck at the New Orleans mint, graded MS-63. Ninety percent silver, a hundred and forty years of pockets and vaults behind it.
The newest resident of the coin tray, and one of the cleaner strikes in the set.

meta.note: Authored narrative copy, keyed by item id. Items without an entry fall back to generated provenance text in the client.
